Let me reconsider the mechanics. Looking at the white regions:
- White at cols 50-57 (rows 30-57) - tall, rightmost
- White at cols 38-47 (rows 52-57) - medium
- White at cols 6-13 (rows 54-57) - short
- White at cols 16-25 (rows 54-57) - medium (after merge)
- White at cols 30-33 (rows 56-57) - short (after shrink)
The white regions seem to be "platforms" that the colored markers sit on. The blue blocks control the height of these platforms.
Looking at the colored markers:
- Track 8 (cols 52-54): charcoal at 27-28, yellow at 29 - sits on white at cols 50-57 (top at row 30)
- Track 5 (cols 36-37): purple at 39 - sits on black bar at 40-57
- Track 2 (cols 14-15): green at 41 - sits on black bar at 42-57
- Track 7 (cols 48-49): yellow at 47 - sits on black bar at 48-57
- Track 6 (cols 41-43): charcoal at 49-50, purple at 51 - sits on white at cols 38-47 (top at row 52)
- Track 1 (cols 8-10): charcoal at 51-52, green at 53 - sits on white at cols 6-13 (top at row 54)
The pattern seems to be that the colored markers are stacked above the white regions or black bars. The black bars have gaps where the colored markers are.
Wait, let me look at the black bars more carefully:
- Black at cols 14-15: rows 38-40 (above green) and 42-57 (below green)
- Black at cols 36-37: rows 36-38 (above purple) and 40-57 (below purple)
- Black at cols 48-49: rows 30-46 (above yellow) and 48-57 (below yellow)
- Black at cols 26-27: rows 52-57 (no gap)
The black bars have gaps where the colored markers are. The markers are "floating" in the gaps.
Now, the white regions:
- White at cols 6-13: rows 54-57 (track 1 sits on top)
- White at cols 16-25: rows 54-57 (track 2 sits on top? No, track 2 is at cols 14-15)
- White at cols 30-33: rows 56-57 (track 3 sits on top? Track 3 is at cols 26-27)
- White at cols 38-47: rows 52-57 (track 6 sits on top)
- White at cols 50-57: rows 30-57 (track 8 sits on top)
Hmm, the white regions don't directly correspond to the tracks. Let me think about this differently.
Actually, I think the white regions are the "ground" and the black bars are "walls" with gaps. The colored markers are "objects" that need to be moved to specific positions.
Looking at the initial state (before any clicks in level 3):
- White at cols 6-13: rows 54-57
- White at cols 18-23: rows 56-57
- White at cols 28-35: rows 54-57
- White at cols 38-47: rows 52-57
- White at cols 50-57: rows 30-57
After clicking blue at cols 12-13 (up):

- White at cols 18-23: disappeared
After clicking blue at cols 16-17 (down):

- White at cols 18-23: rows 56-57 (reappeared)
After clicking blue at cols 24-25:

- White at cols 30-33: rows 56-57 (shrank from cols 28-35)
So the blue blocks seem to control the horizontal extent of the white regions, not just vertical movement. This is getting complicated.
Let me try a completely different approach. Let me look at the initial state of level 3 and see if there's a clear goal state. Maybe the goal is to make all white regions the same height, or to create a continuous white platform.
Actually, looking at the white regions:
- cols 50-57: rows 30-57 (height 28)
- cols 38-47: rows 52-57 (height 6)
- cols 6-13: rows 54-57 (height 4)
- cols 16-25: rows 54-57 (height 4)
- cols 30-33: rows 56-57 (height 2)
The heights are different. Maybe the goal is to make them all the same height?
Or maybe the goal is related to the colored markers. Let me look at the colored markers again:
- Track 8: charcoal at 27-28, yellow at 29 (highest)
- Track 5: purple at 39
- Track 2: green at 41
- Track 7: yellow at 47
- Track 6: charcoal at 49-50, purple at 51
- Track 1: charcoal at 51-52, green at 53
The colored markers are at different heights. Maybe the goal is to align them all at the same height by moving the white regions up or down.
Actually, I just realized something. The white regions might be "lifts" that move the colored markers up or down. When I clicked the blue block at cols 12-13, the white region at cols 6-13 expanded upward, and the colored markers at cols 8-10 moved up with it.
So the mechanics are:
- Blue blocks control the vertical extent of adjacent white regions
- The colored markers move with the white regions
- The goal is to align the colored markers at specific heights
Let me think about what the target heights might be. Looking at the black bars:

- Black at cols 36-37: gap at row 39 (purple marker)
- Black at cols 48-49: gap at row 47 (yellow marker)
- Black at cols 26-27: no gap (just a solid bar)
